solution:

```python
class KthLargest:

    def __init__(self, k: int, nums: List[int]):
        self.k = k
        self.nums = nums
        self.index = len(nums) - k + 1

    def add(self, val: int) -> int:
        if len(self.nums) < self.index:
            self.nums.append(val)
            self.index -= 1
        return self.nums[self.index]

# Your KthLargest object will be instantiated and called as follows:
# obj = KthLargest(k, nums)
# result = obj.add(val)
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:

The provided solution uses a class to maintain the index of the kth largest element in the list of test scores. When a new score is added, the index is updated accordingly. The time complexity is O(1) because the function only makes a constant number of operations, and the space complexity is O(1) because the class only uses a single integer variable to store the index. The energy implications are low because the solution only performs a few constant operations and uses minimal resources.
